item.title: Guyatone EB-9 Sharp 5 Bass 1968 - 1998 - Blue — $1,250
item.description:
$1,250 · Excellent · Clingan Guitar Tone

The Guyatone EB-9 Sharp 5 Bass is a wonderfully quirky short-scale from Japan’s golden era, with plenty of vibe and personality built right in. This example has a replaced pickguard and is fitted with a 1960s Yamaha bass mini humbucker, giving it a unique tonal flavour that sets it apart from stock models. In excellent overall, it’s a cool, characterful piece that blends collectability with one-of-a-kind playability.

All instruments sold by Clingan Guitar Tone are inspected, serviced, and set up by our professionally trained guitar and amp technicians, ensuring they perform at their absolute best. Our team’s expertise and attention to detail mean you can buy with complete confidence, knowing your gear has been given the highest level of care.

Any questions, don't hesitate to ask!
Located at Clingan Guitar Tone, 1/155 Donald Street, Brunswick East Melbourne
Available for viewing and playing anytime. We ship all around the world, so feel free to ask for an accurate shipping quote

Country of origin: Japan

item.title: Lefthanded Tokai Hard Puncher 1979 - sunburst — $1,255.39
item.description:
$1,255.39 · Good · CC Shop

Very rare lefthanded Tokai Hard Puncher in good condition strung with flatwounds.

Date is stamped on the back of the headstock in gold, trussrod is on the heel of the neck and it wears a thick black script logo. If we rely on the serial number it is a vintage 1979 bass, stamped on the back of the headstock and the trussrod being on the heel of the neck it could be a 2000 reissue. Either way it is a Japan made bass, highly sought after. The bass is in good condition, everything works as it should and it has that vintage growl. Comes with a case, good protected.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Studio-4 bass in Honey Satin — $1,149
item.description:
$1,149 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Stiletto Studio-4 Electric Bass Honey Satin

The Stiletto Studio-4 delivers a comfortable, fast neck experience defined by its thin 'C' profile and 24 X-Jumbo frets across a 34" scale. The Honey Satin finish pairs warm aesthetics with practical durability, while the neck-through construction with ultra access ensures stability and uncompromised upper-fret playability. This is a serious working bass engineered for session work, studio recording, and gigging musicians who demand responsive feel and consistent performance.

Playability & Build

The Maple/Walnut multi-ply neck with Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut and 2-way adjustable truss rod provides a stable foundation for the fretboard's 16" radius and 24 X-Jumbo frets. The thin 'C' neck shape—measuring .787" at the 1st fret and .866" at the 12th fret—facilitates rapid position changes and cross-string technique, while the abalone offset dot inlays and Schecter tuners round out a professionally executed specification.

Tone & Electronics

Dual EMG 35HZ pickups paired with 3-Band EMG Active EQ and master volume/blend controls deliver modern clarity and cut, with the 18-volt system providing extended headroom for articulate response across the frequency spectrum. The Diamond Custom Bass bridge and Bubinga top over mahogany body contribute focused midrange and defined sustain characteristic of neck-through construction.

Key Features
  • Neck-Through construction with Ultra Access upper-fret design
  • Mahogany body with Bubinga top and Honey Satin finish
  • Maple/Walnut multi-ply neck with thin 'C' profile
  • 34" scale length with 24 X-Jumbo frets
  • Rosewood fretboard with abalone offset dot inlays
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (1.496" width)
  • Dual EMG 35HZ pickups with 3-Band EMG Active EQ
  • 18-volt active electronics with screw-plate battery compartment
  • Diamond Custom Bass bridge with satin gold hardware
  • 2-way adjustable truss rod with 5/32" Allen nut
  • Fretboard radius: 16" (406mm)
  • Schecter tuners and metal knurled control knobs

The Stiletto Studio-4 combines precise engineering with pro-grade components, delivering a versatile instrument suited to any bass player prioritizing playability and tonal flexibility.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay Vintage Sunburst 1991 Electric Bass Guitar — $2,265.36
item.description:
$2,265.36 · Good · Sell Us Your Guitar

Here for sale we have a Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay in Vintage Sunburst. Made in the USA in 1991 this electric bass guitar features an Ash body, Birds Eye Maple neck and Rosewood fingerboard. Other appointments include a Hipshot Drop D tuning machine and MusicMan Chrome Plated bridge with adjustable mute pads. An aftermarket Nordstrand MM4.2 dual coil

has been fitted and is wired to a master volume and 3-band EQ.


The Birds Eye Maple neck plays very well, with a profile that might be described as a moderately-sized 'C' shape which sits comfortably in the hand and provides a smooth and responsive playing experience across the register. The Rosewood fingerboard provides a smooth and comfortable playing surface, along with the 1.68” nut width which allows for different techniques such as slap bass or chordal playing.  The Gloss finish to the rear assists with slick navigation of the 34" scale, and along with the generous cutaways ensures that access to the upper frets is a breeze. 


The Nordstrand MM4.2 Dual Coil provides a driving deep growl, with pronounced punchy mids and a snappy treble response which blends perfectly into a wide range of musical settings; filling out the low end of the mix and capturing all the attack and sparkle of your playing. The 3-band EQ and master volume controls allows you to dial in your perfect tone right on the instrument. 


Brand- Music Man

Model- Stingray

Finish- Vintage Sunburst

Year- 1991

Made In- USA

Body- Ash

Neck- Maple

Fingerboard- Rosewood

Frets- 21

Scale Legnth- 34"

Nut Width- 1.68"

Pickups- Nordstrand MM4.2 dual coil

Controls- Volume, bass, mid, treble


Luthier Report


Our ID: 1030726221150

Weight: 10.25lbs  

Serial: 32806


Aesthetic Condition:

Headstock: Overall very good condition. The top edge of the headstock demonstrates several indentations and chipping to the finish.

Neck: Overall excellent condition. The rear of the neck demonstrates some very small indentation under close scrutiny, these cannot be felt undehand when playing.

Body: Overall good condition. All surfaces of the body demonstrate light scratching and several light indentations. These are most prominent on the bass side of the front face towards the upper horn and waist. There is a slightly larger indentation on the bass side lower edge which exposes the wood beneath. There is another small chip which exposes the wood located on the edge of the treble side upper horn. A slight hazing of the finish is apparent throughout. A very small finish crack can be seen to the treble side neck pocket.


Condition of Frets:

The frets demonstrate moderate indentations throughout however these do not effect palyability. There is material remaining for any future fret work the new owner may wish to perform.


Current Set Up:

Truss Rod: Fully functional. Truss rod tool (spoke wheel) not included.

Hardware: Fully functional and good condition. All of the hardware demonstrates tarnishing, particularly the tuners and the bridge. A hipshot D-Tuner has been installed. The Foam has been removed from the adjustable mute pads.

Structural: No structural concerns to note.


Height of the string from the 12th Fret-2mm - Bass 2.5mm


Please be aware that the action on a guitar may move slightly in transit and this measurement is the current height.


Strings on Guitar - 45-100


Electrics: Fully functional. The pickup has been replaced with a Nordstrand MM4.2 dual coil.


Case & Accessories

Original EBMM Hard Case

Overall excellent condition with signs of wear to the outer case.


Please be aware that for international buyers import fees are not included in our postage and packaging cost, this only covers the cost of the guitar being sent to you. Import fees will be charged by Customs upon the guitar's arrival in the recipient country. Due to Brexit, this also applies to EU buyers. We advise checking the total import cost with Customs as this is not information we are able to accurately provide

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5 H with Rosewood Fretboard 1999 - 2018 - Dark Cherry Burst — $2,331.44
item.description:
$2,331.44 · Excellent · Martin's Boutique

Bonjour à tous,

Afin de financer l’achat d’une autre basse, je vends cette superbe Sting Ray 5 en état irréprochable pour une seconde main.

Le Black cherry burst peut paraitre presque noir à défaut d’éclairage.
Un petit LEGO est collé sur la plaque comme le montrent les photos, j’ai eu la basse comme ça, je trouve ça très pratique et assez original personnellement.

A part ça, finitions nickel, pas un poc,, le son Sting Rray comme attendu.

Les Music Man ne se trouvent plus du tout sur le marché du neuf, la marque ayant décidé que les gauchers ne seraient plus leurs clients…

item.title: 【used】Aria Pro II / RSB DELUXE-II White 1985 3.965kg #5020178【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $591
item.description:
$591 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Aria Pro II is an international brand that has been active since the early days of guitar making in Japan.

As a brand of Arai Trading, a large trading company, it excels at combining high quality with accessible pricing.

This is a superb example from the highly popular RSB series.

The body is made of alder, the neck is maple, and the fingerboard is rosewood, a classic wood combination.

The neck pickup is a powerful split-coil pickup, and the bridge pickup is a hum-canceling pickup.

The neck pickup can be switched between series and parallel wiring, and the bridge pickup can be selected to operate as a single-coil or hum-canceling pickup.

This allows for a wide range of tones, from powerful and fat sounds to sharp and clear sounds.

This is a guitar full of the charm of Japanese vintage guitars.

The frets have approximately 70% remaining.

The truss rod is stiff, but it has been confirmed to move about 90 degrees.

The neck is currently adjusted to GIB standards (2.0mm-2.5mm at the 12th fret), and there is slight fret lift at the last fret, causing a slight buzz when picking strongly.

The body and paintwork show overall minor scratches. There is a noticeable scratch approximately 30.0mm in diameter on the back of the body. The wood grain is partially chipped around the truss rod adjustment area. There is a paint chip of approximately 7.0mm near the neck joint. There is paint chipping near the tuning machines for the 1st, 3rd, and 4th strings. There is a paint chip of approximately 20.0mm at the tip of the headstock.

The electronics are in good condition.

The neck pickup volume/tone and bridge pickup volume/tone pots have been replaced with current CTS pots.

Controls: Neck pickup volume/tone, neck pickup wiring switch (parallel/series), bridge pickup wiring switch (tap/hum-canceling), bridge pickup volume/tone, pickup selector switch
Nut width: 40.5mm
Bridge string spacing: 18.0mm
Scale length: 34 inches
Year of manufacture: 1985
Country of manufacture: JAPAN

GIB craftsmen will adjust the instrument for easy playability before delivery.

Accessories: Soft case

item.title: Dingwall NG3-5 Adam Nolly Getgood Signature Matte Celestial Blue — $2,988.06
item.description:
$2,988.06 · Excellent · Drop-D Sounds

Based on the Combustion model, the alder body, maple neck and fretboard NG3 takes things in an exciting new direction with the addition of the custom Darkglass Tone Capsule pre-amp, carbon fiber print pickguard and racing/tuner inspired finishes.

Condition: New / B-Stock - Very minor paint chip near neckjoint.

  • Made in China/Canada (Hybrid Manufacturing)
  • Body: Alder
  • Neck: 5-piece maple, bolt-on
  • Fingerboard: Maple, 24 ‘banjo-sized’ frets, 9.45-inch radius

  • Preamp: Darkglass Tone Capsule
  • Controls: Master volume, 4-Position Quad-Tone pickup selector, 3-Band Active EQ (Bass, Low-Mid, High-Mid), Active/Passive Toggle Switch
  • Bridge: Dingwall ‘Minimalist’ one-piece bridge

  • Tuners: Licensed Hipshot Ultralite
  • Nut: Graphtech Black Tusq
  • Pickguard: Carbon-fibre print
  • Case: Dingwall Padded Gigbag

item.title: 【used】Ernie Ball Music Man / Stingray VT NAT 1996 4.980kg #B016005【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,953
item.description:
$2,953 · Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Musicman emerged as Leo Fender's second brand and was loved by bassists worldwide.
Even after being acquired by Ernie Ball, this remains unchanged, and it continues to be widely accepted by modern bassists.

Many artists, including Louis Johnson and Flea, are known to use Musicman basses. (Titles omitted)

The Stingray is a classic model that boasts overwhelming popularity within the brand.

Alongside Fender's Jazz Bass and Precision Bass, it's safe to say it's one of the most popular basses in the world.

This is a Japanese-made Stingray VT.

While the EX is widely known as a Japanese-made Stingray, this model is its predecessor.

It was manufactured for a very short period and is rare.

Manufactured in 1996.

The body is likely made of ash.

The neck and fretboard are maple.

The pickups and preamp are original.

In terms of specifications, it follows the Pre Ernie's specifications, with a 2-band preamp and string-through-body bridge.

It features a distinctive, masculine design with a single humbucking pickup mounted at the bridge.

The punchy sound can only be obtained with this guitar.

It's a must-try for anyone playing funk or mixture genres.

The frets have approximately 60% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork show some minor scratches and small dents. There is some tarnishing on the metal parts.

The electronics are in good condition.

An additional truss rod groove has been added. The pickguard has also been modified accordingly. The pickups have been replaced with Seymour Duncan Antiquity II Jive pickups.

Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass
Nut Width: 43.8mm
Bridge String Spacing: 19.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 1996
Country of Manufacture: Japan

During setup, we use our original GIB BASIC / Standard Bass Strings.

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.

These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

Please enjoy these strings, which aim to be a new standard for bassists.

They are delivered after being adjusted for easy playability by GIB's craftsmen.

item.title: Sire Marcus Miller P5 4-string Electric Bass 2026 - Mild Green — $598
item.description:
$598 · Brand New · Atkins Guitars

Sire Marcus Miller P5 4-String Electric Bass Guitar

DEMO: Open Box

When the timeless design of the P-style bass meets the playing specifications of the legendary Marcus Miller, you can anticipate an instrument born of vintage flair, modern sensibilities, and professional-grade features. It's no surprise that the Sire Marcus Miller P5 4-string bass exceeds all expectations. Delivering a premium bass guitar built of solid North American alder, a 1-piece roasted hard maple neck, and a complementary maple fingerboard, this bass is a tonal chameleon, adaptable to a number of genres and styles, both old school and contemporary. Powered by a punchy, tone-rich Marcus Vintage-Fat Precision Revolution split-coil pickup and loaded with pro-level features like Sire’s rolled fingerboard edges, a Marcus Miller Vintage-S bridge, and a real bone nut, the Marcus Miller P5 is a true workhorse. Built for players of all levels, this bass delivers a commanding low end that’s ready for the studio, the stage, and anywhere the groove takes you.

A wealth of maple

Many players certify that a thoughtfully designed neck is the hallmark of a quality instrument, and the Sire Marcus Miller P5 further solidifies this conviction. This neck features a 1-piece roasted hard maple construction for vintage tone and old-school vibe, topped with a roasted hard maple fretboard, a 9.5-inch radius, 34-inch scale, and rolled edges for maximum playing comfort. At once sleek, smooth, and substantial, it’s no wonder the Marcus Miller P5 has become the go-to axe for many a discerning bassists.

Simplicity reigns supreme

To be a true working musician’s tool, a bass needs versatile pickups and intuitive controls that can handle any style with ease, and the Marcus Miller P5 nails it. Housing a punchy, authoritative Marcus Vintage-Fat Precision Revolution pickup voiced by Marcus Miller himself, the P5 handles everything from retro thump to modern slap with a confident approach and refined dynamics. And with a simple Volume/Tone control setup, you won’t spend your set adjusting a litany of knobs trying to dial in a suitable tone; the Marcus Miller P5 allows you to quickly find and express your authentic sound, letting the music guide every performance.

Sire Marcus Miller P5 4-string Features:

  • Iconic P-style design meets hand-selected specs for the performance needs of the legendary Marcus Miller
  • Lightweight North American alder body delivers old-school sound with prominent upper mids 
  • 1-piece roasted hard maple neck with C-shape profile provides a comfortable playing experience for a variety of genres and styles
  • Roasted hard maple fingerboard with rolled edges designed for speed and smooth navigation
  • Marcus Vintage-Fat Precision Revolution pickup delivers a thick, versatile punch 
  • Easily adjust your sound with simple volume and tone knobs
  • Real bone nut and Marcus Miller Vintage-S bridge further enhance the professional sound and design

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.

It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorius, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee (titles omitted).

The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.

Its sharp, clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to enjoy overwhelming support and a shining presence even today.

This model is a cost-effective Jazz Bass from the Mexican-made Player series.

The body is made of alder.

The fingerboard is made of Pau Ferro.

The pickups are Player Series Alnico 5 Single-Coil pickups, specially designed for this series.

They deliver a punchy attack and a genuine Fender tone that leads the ensemble.

Perhaps due to the Mexican manufacturing environment, the sound has a slightly different, more open and dynamic feel compared to Japanese-made models.

While based on the classic Jazz Bass sound, this bass incorporates modern updates such as a flatter fingerboard radius, resulting in exceptional playability.

Don't miss this opportunity!

Fret life is approximately 80%.

Truss rod is in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

item.description:
$5,014 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Riggio Custom Guitars is an American guitar/bass brand.


The builder, Joe Riggio, is also highly regarded as a repairman.


They specialize in vintage styles, and all their instruments are handmade.


This is a traditional PB type.


The body is made of alder, and the fretboard is made of Madagascar rosewood—a classic combination.


As can be seen from the texture of the fretboard, high-quality wood is used.


Attention to detail is evident in the nitrocellulose lacquer finish, clay dots, and relicing.


The pickups have been replaced with Olinto brand pickups.


This is also quite rare.


The strings bounce on the fretboard, and the sound spreads throughout the instrument, creating a pleasant playing feel that makes you want to keep playing.


As expected of a PB, the thick, open nuance is the universally valuable sound of a bass.


The frets have approximately 90% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show dents, scratches, paint chipping, and weather checking, but due to the aged finish, it's impossible to determine if these are later additions. The metal parts also show some tarnishing. Two screw holes for a finger rest are visible.


The electronics are in good condition.


The pickups have been replaced with Olinto pickups.


Controls: Volume, Tone

Nut Width: 42.9mm

Bridge String Spacing: 20.0mm

Scale Length: 34 Inch

These strings are characterized by their straightforward sound and stable playability, naturally bringing out the instrument's inherent character.


These are highly reliable strings that we actually use in our repair and setup workshops.

item.title: 【used】Sadowsky / MX20 UV5 TBT/R 2026 3.835kg #SMX A 000273-26【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $1,265
item.description:
$1,265 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Sadowsky is a high-end New York brand with top-tier musicians such as Marcus Miller and Will Lee as clients. (Titles omitted)

Previously, the Metroline Express was manufactured in Japan, but as Warwick took over the distribution of Sadowsky products, manufacturing shifted to a factory in China.

While keeping the selling price down, high quality is maintained, making it a very unique product in its price range.

This is the MX20 UV5, the first PB-type guitar in the MX series.

The body is made of lightweight solid Okoume, the neck is made of Roasted Maple, and the fingerboard is made of Morado (Pau Ferro) or Roasted Maple.

This is a standard combination in the MX series.

The construction has been thoroughly considered for excellent body balance and sound projection, resulting in a very refined balance that is comfortable to hold.

The back of the neck has a satin finish, providing a smooth and pleasant feel.

The pickup is a "Sadowsky passive P-Style pickup."

It has the powerful, forceful projection characteristic of PB-type basses, yet delivers a clear sound without muddy bass tones.

The preamp features the familiar 2-band EQ.

You can significantly boost the bass and treble frequencies with the touch of a button.

The circuit board of this preamp is almost identical to that used in the top-of-the-line models priced around 1 million yen.

Copper foil shielding is also applied, resulting in surprisingly low noise.

These points are extremely significant advantages when choosing an MX series bass.

Even in this price range, you can see Roger Sadowsky's uncompromising dedication to instrument making.

While offering the ultra-smooth playability characteristic of Sadowsky, the sound is unmistakably that of a P-bass.

It outputs a modern tone that beautifully blends a robust punch with sweet, airy, and clear treble tones.

This guitar embodies Roger Sadowsky's philosophy, blending a traditional PB design with modern elements.

The frets have approximately 90% remaining.

The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork are generally clean, although there are some minor scratches.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Volume, Treble, Bass
Nut Width: 47.5mm
Bridge String Spacing: 18.0mm
Scale Length: 34 Inch
Year of Manufacture: 2026
Country of Manufacture: CHINA

During setup, we used D'addario EXL strings, highly trusted by professional musicians.

Characterized by a powerful attack and rich harmonics, these strings truly deserve to be called a standard.

These high-quality strings bring out the instrument's performance and character to the fullest, so please enjoy them as well.

GIB's craftsmen will adjust the instrument for optimal playability before delivery.

item.description:
$1,131.96 · Very Good · TC GAKKI

Welcome to TC Gakki! All photos are of the item for sale. Please note they are for sale in our store and may sell out. Thank you for looking! Feel free to ask any questions!

Product Details
________________________________

The Heritage series faithfully reproduces vintage details!

The Heritage series joins the Fender Made In Japan lineup, following the Traditional and Hybrid series.

This series is produced under the supervision of Mark Kendrik, a former Master Builder at the Fender Custom Shop and a master of vintage instruments.

Based on profiling data from USA products, the body and neck shapes are designed to match each era, reproducing the look and feel down to the smallest detail. Recommended for those who prefer vintage style.

This instrument is designed according to 60s specifications, with an alder body and slab rosewood fingerboard.

It delivers a powerful bass sound with a gritty attack. The tone is characterized by a calm, understated sound rather than a harsh, flashy one.

With its lacquer finish, this is a guitar whose look and sound will change beautifully over time!

item.title: 【used】Fender Japan / JB62-75 CAR 1983 4.030kg #JV60286【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,202
item.description:
$2,202 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Fender is, of course, the number one brand of electric guitars and basses.


It's loved by countless top artists, including Jaco Pastorious, Marcus Miller, and Geddy Lee. (Titles omitted)


The Jazz Bass was released as a higher-end model than the Precision Bass.


Its sharp, clear sound is loved by many bassists, and it continues to receive overwhelming support and shines brightly even today.


Fender Japan started in the 1980s as a Japanese-made Fender, but unfortunately, it has now been discontinued and integrated into Fender itself.


However, the brand's quality is still supported by many artists and continues to be sought after.


This model is the classic JB62-75.


Manufactured in 1983.


The neck end has ""6-29'83"" handwritten on it.


The model was determined based on the specifications.


It is said that Fender Japan models differ depending on the serial number, but this one is the legendary and popular JV serial number. The body is made of alder, and the fingerboard is rosewood—a popular combination.


It features a spiral bridge and reverse-wound tuning machines, a truly classic specification.


The pickups have been replaced with Seymour Duncan Antiquity II Jive pickups.


This is clearly an upgrade.


The quality is superb, eloquently speaking of the excellence of Fender Japan's JV era in terms of sound and playability.


The powerful low end, crisp treble, and rich yet sharp sound are universally wonderful, as expected of a JB.


The frets have approximately 90% remaining.


The truss rod is in good condition.


The neck is in good condition.


The body and paintwork show fine scratches and minor dents throughout. There is a paint chip of about 1 cm on the elbow contour. There are several paint chips of about 0.5 mm on the side of the body. There is a paint crack of about 6.5 cm near the neck joint plate. A sticker is attached to the side of the neck.


The electronics are in good condition.


The pickguard has been replaced. A routing has been done in the body to allow easier access to the truss rod. The pickguard has also been modified accordingly.


Controls: Neck pickup volume, bridge pickup volume, master tone

Nut width: 39.0mm

Bridge string spacing: 20.0mm

Scale: 34 inches

Year of manufacture: 1983

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reuse/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------
This is a signature model of Jerry Barnes, the bassist of CHIC.

ATELIER Z is a long-established Japanese brand that enjoys widespread support from both professional and amateur musicians across various genres. This particular model is a signature model of Jerry Burns, the bassist of the American funk/disco band CHIC.

Based on a mid-70s JB-type bass, it features a lightweight 3-piece ash body and a 3-bolt joint. Compared to the high-density, edgy sound of ATELIER Z's flagship model "M#245," it is characterized by a light, airy acoustic resonance with a pleasant sense of space and depth. It also features the company's EQ-M preamp. In addition to sound correction that takes advantage of the passive sound, it is also possible to create a robust and powerful sound by boosting both the treble and bass. The bass has a soft, understated low end that seems to fit well in vocal ensembles. The bass itself has a moderate amount of compression, so it produces a cohesive sound whether fingerpicking or slapping. This characteristic also makes it surprisingly compatible with pick playing.

This bass guitar offers a funky, crisp sound with a great sense of speed. It's also recommended for those looking for a lightweight and easy-to-use bass. As it's a made-to-order model, opportunities to try out the actual instrument in-store will likely become rare in the future. Please come and try it out!

Weight: ≈3.75kg
S/N: #042394

【Specifications】
Neck: Maple 1P 20F
Body: Ash3P
Tuning key: GOTOH GB-528
Bridge: Vintage-type
Pickups: ATELIER Z JBZ-4/70' Premium(70'SET)
Preamp: ATELIER Z EQ-M
Controls: FVol., RVol./sw, Treble, Bass
Color: VN (Vintage Natural)

Gig bag included

-----------------------------------

[Manufacturer Outlet / Slightly Damaged Item at a Special Price]
As this is a manufacturer's outlet item, there may be some scratches and dents, but this does not affect playability.

The "Hermes" series features passive models that faithfully reproduce the instrument's natural tone.

Kikuchi Guitars was born to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Yoshiyuki Kikuchi, one of Japan's leading luthiers, visiting the world-renowned contemporary master craftsman Roger Sadowsky. You can fully experience the depth of his understanding of instruments, which he has cultivated while faithfully inheriting Sadowsky's spirit.

This "Hermes" series is the brand's standard line. While the basic specifications and manufacturing quality are almost the same as the custom models, the regular model lineup with fixed specifications achieves high cost performance.

This instrument is a pure passive model equipped with a newly developed pickup that converts the instrument's inherent resonance, created by the precise assembly of the body and neck, into an electrical signal without spoiling it. Combined with simple controls, the newly developed pickup produces a warm, natural tone that envelops the listener, characteristic of passive guitars. It captures the nuances of picking without compromising them, bringing out the player's individuality to the fullest, from groovy playing to percussive techniques like slap bass. Please try the world-class sound and playability of "Kikuchi Guitars," a brand that can be called the culmination of Mr. Kikuchi's knowledge and experience from Sadowsky and Three Dots Guitars.

- Pickups -
The pickups, newly developed specifically for the Passive model, are designed with a vintage feel that emphasizes the separation of each string's tone and natural warmth, and the volume and tone balance have been adjusted to ensure that each string is seamlessly connected.

- Controls -
It features a classic passive pickup configuration with 2 Volume and 1 Tone controls. The pickup captures every nuance of your picking, and by adjusting the volume balance of each control, you can create a wide range of tonal variations.

- Body -
The Dinky shape, redesigned to allow for comfortable playing even for those with a Japanese physique, is adopted. The body, without a routed back panel, amplifies the natural resonance produced by the wood.

The RV model features alder wood. In addition to the warm, enveloping tone of the rosewood fingerboard, it is characterized by a pleasant bass response.

- Neck -
The neck has been strengthened and the headstock has been made thicker to create the optimal resonance and weight balance for playing.

With a narrower nut width than typical J-style guitars, and a neck grip that retains a moderate thickness for durability and playability while being easy to hold, you can play smoothly from the low to high positions, and even mute the wide 5th string.

Furthermore, by using nickel frets with a higher hardness, the noise generated when pressing down on the frets is reduced, producing a punchy sound despite being made of nickel. 22 frets are used to accommodate a wide range of musical genres. By avoiding excessive fret extension and designing the neck joint position deeper towards the bridge, it can be played with a feel similar to a 21-fret bass.

To accommodate various playing techniques and musical genres, the string height is set uniformly at every fret position, resulting in a powerful tone for forceful picking and a warm, enveloping tone for gentle fingering.

*The image is a sample.

【SPECIFICATIONS】
Model: 4st
Body: Alder 2-Piece (Solid)
Neck: Maple, Thin Matte Nitro
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Fingerboard Radius: 12inch (305R)
Nut Material: Tusq
Nut Width: 37mm
Fret: DHP24H, 22F
Scale: 864mm (34)
Body Finish: Poly
Hardware Color: Chrome
Bridge: Hipshot 20mm Spacing
Tuner: Hipshot License Ultra-Light
Pickups:Original Custom J (4st)
Matches:
Volume (Neck)
Volume (Bridge)
Tone
String: D'Addario Nickel .045 - .105

Includes semi-hard case

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reuse/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------
The M#265 is a custom-order model with an ebony fingerboard and an 18mm pitch.

The M (Magnitude) bass guitar enjoys immense popularity among bassists, both professional and amateur, especially those who specialize in slap bass techniques. Not only is it Atelier Z's flagship model, it's also arguably one of the most representative Japanese-made active basses, boasting a level of recognition that has endured for many years. Based on the style of 70's JB basses, it incorporates Atelier Z's unique ideas throughout its construction, and its high level of craftsmanship, with attention to detail from a bassist's perspective, is the secret to its popularity. This particular model, the M#265, is a 5-string model based on the M#245, featuring a 34-inch scale length and an 18mm string spacing.

This guitar features a high-quality ash body. The body design reflects feedback from musicians, and despite its asymmetrical JB shape, the smoother shape on the control side minimizes slippage and tipping when placed on a stand. The maple neck and ebony fingerboard, which are characterized by their powerful low end, are fitted with celluloid-wrapped necks and block position markers. The original bridge, which provides ample sustain and a rich attack, is modeled after the high-quality bridges that were once very popular. In addition, the use of a 3mm thick clear pickguard reduces the distance between the strings and the pickguard, and combined with the half-cut pickup fence, it provides stability for slap bass techniques, thumbing and pulling. The Tuning Z System, with its moderate tension and low string height setting, also provides smooth playability. These player-friendly specifications and meticulous adjustments are likely the secrets to its long-standing popularity.

Even with a low action setting, it delivers a solid, punchy sound that doesn't lose its body, producing a strong bass presence that stands out in various ensembles and genres. The preamp is the familiar Bartolini/XTCT, which fully utilizes the rich resonance of the bass itself while enabling even more aggressive sound shaping. When used actively, it can produce a wide range of sounds, from thick, rocking bass to scooped slap sounds with a boosted EQ. Furthermore, the instrument itself has a rich sound with ample mass, so even when used passively, it produces harmonically rich and elastic bass tones, making it suitable for various playing styles and musical genres.

This particular instrument is a custom-ordered model commissioned by our shop. It features a transparent blue matching headstock. Its appearance evokes the image of a simplified version of a renowned endorser's instrument. Beyond its instantly recognizable, distinctive sound, the meticulous quality control of our workshop's basses provides a sense of security that captivates many bassists. This is a truly professional model that will make a statement on stage and in recording studios. Don't miss this opportunity to get your hands on one!

Weight: ≈4.51kg
S/N: #042386

【Specifications】
[Neck]
Scale: 34 inches (864 mm)
Neck Material: Maple 1P
Fingerboard Material: Ebony
Fingerboard Radius: 400R

Nut Width: 47.5mm
Finish: Fingerboard&Head top/Gloss，Neck back/Mat
[Body]

[Electronics]

Hermes has released the long-awaited full-size and 20F 70 series!

The 70 Series has been newly created, with its full-size body, 20-fret neck, and 70's-style pickup placement all blending together in a "perfect" way to produce a distinctive tone.

Unlike the versatile specifications of the standard Hermes series, this model incorporates traditional 70s specifications in various parts, resulting in rich lows and crisp highs. The combination of two volume controls allows for precise control of the volume of each pickup, greatly expanding the range of tonal variations that players can create.

- Neck -
The 20-fret fingerboard produces a crisp, snappy, and punchy tone when using slap techniques.

The slightly strongly curved 10-inch radius fretboard makes it easy to produce pleasing ghost notes, allowing you to play the powerful "chopper tone" that many players envision.

To accommodate various playing techniques and musical genres, the string height is set uniformly at every fret position, resulting in a powerful tone for forceful picking and a warm, enveloping tone for gentle fingering.

- Body -
Featuring a full-size body that can handle sharp and powerful inputs, it produces a dazzling tone. It boasts the boundless richness of bass characteristic of a full-size instrument, creating a thick, warm, and natural woody resonance.

The body is made of ash wood. The dry, punchy tone blends perfectly with the rich, powerful tone of the full-size shape.

- Pickups -
The bridge pickup utilizes a 70's-style position, which brings it closer to the bridge. When mixed with the neck pickup, it produces a clear and crisp tone.

Furthermore, when fingerpicking with your fingers resting on the bridge pickup, your picking position naturally shifts towards the bridge, allowing you to produce a tight, well-defined tone.

- Controls -
Instead of a pickup balancer, it employs a classic 2-volume control. This allows for precise adjustment of each pickup's volume, greatly expanding the range of tonal variations obtainable when mixing pickups.

*The image is a sample.

Hermes, crafted to be accessible to a wider audience while fully showcasing Kikuchi's masterful skills, now features a model combining an ash body with a rosewood fingerboard.

Kikuchi Guitars was born to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Yoshiyuki Kikuchi, one of Japan's leading luthiers, visiting the world-renowned contemporary master craftsman Roger Sadowsky. You can fully experience the depth of his understanding of instruments, which he has cultivated while faithfully inheriting Sadowsky's spirit.

This "Hermes" series is the brand's standard line. While the basic specifications and manufacturing quality are almost the same as the custom models, the regular model lineup with fixed specifications achieves high cost performance.

Experience the world-class sound and playability of "Kikuchi Guitars," a brand that can be considered the culmination of Mr. Kikuchi's knowledge and experience gained at Sadowsky and Three Dots Guitars.

- Neck -
The neck has been strengthened and the headstock thickened to create the optimal resonance and weight balance for playing. The nut width is narrower than typical J-style guitars, while the neck grip retains a moderate thickness for durability and playability, making it easy to hold. This allows for smooth playing from the low to high positions, and even smooth muting.

Furthermore, by using nickel frets with a higher hardness, the noise generated when pressing down on the frets is reduced, producing a punchy sound despite being made of nickel. The number of frets is 22F to accommodate a wide range of musical genres. By avoiding excessive fret overhang and designing the neck joint position deeper towards the bridge, it can be played with a feel similar to a 21-fret bass.

To accommodate various playing techniques and musical genres, the string height is set uniformly at every fret position, resulting in a powerful tone for forceful picking and a warm, enveloping tone for gentle fingering.

- Body -
The Dinky shape, redesigned to allow for comfortable playing even for those with a Japanese physique, is adopted for this guitar. Not only does it offer easy handling and a modern tone, but the solid body with its appropriate weight produces a clear, articulate tone, creating a wide range of tonal variations.

- Hardware -
The lightweight and highly maneuverable Hipshot parts, also used in the Custom Series, are strategically placed throughout the instrument to directly transmit the characteristics of the wood, resulting in a more natural sound.

- Pickups -
It features a custom DiMarzio pickup, also used in the Custom Series. This pickup delivers a sharp, crisp sound with plenty of power and hum-canceling noise reduction.

- Preamp -
To achieve the ultimate tone that Kikuchi envisions, this guitar features an original preamp developed in collaboration with a world-class design manufacturer, and also used in the Custom Series. When boosted, it delivers a clear and natural tone in the low and high frequencies, and scoops the midrange, while the passive/active switching is designed to minimize the perceived difference in volume and tone.

Hermes, crafted to be accessible to a wider audience while fully showcasing Kikuchi's masterful skills, now features a model combining an ash body with a rosewood fingerboard.

Kikuchi Guitars was born to commemorate the 30th anniversary of Yoshiyuki Kikuchi, one of Japan's leading luthiers, visiting the world-renowned contemporary master craftsman Roger Sadowsky. You can fully experience the depth of his understanding of instruments, which he has cultivated while faithfully inheriting Sadowsky's spirit.

This "Hermes" series is the brand's standard line. While the basic specifications and manufacturing quality are almost the same as the custom models, the regular model lineup with fixed specifications achieves high cost performance.

Experience the world-class sound and playability of "Kikuchi Guitars," a brand that can be considered the culmination of Mr. Kikuchi's knowledge and experience gained at Sadowsky and Three Dots Guitars.

- Neck -
The neck has been strengthened and the headstock thickened to create the optimal resonance and weight balance for playing. The nut width is narrower than typical J-style guitars, while the neck grip retains a moderate thickness for durability and playability, making it easy to hold. This allows for smooth playing from the low to high positions, and even smooth muting.

Furthermore, by using nickel frets with a higher hardness, the noise generated when pressing down on the frets is reduced, producing a punchy sound despite being made of nickel. The number of frets is 22F to accommodate a wide range of musical genres. By avoiding excessive fret overhang and designing the neck joint position deeper towards the bridge, it can be played with a feel similar to a 21-fret bass.

To accommodate various playing techniques and musical genres, the string height is set uniformly at every fret position, resulting in a powerful tone for forceful picking and a warm, enveloping tone for gentle fingering.

- Body -
The Dinky shape, redesigned to allow for comfortable playing even for those with a Japanese physique, is adopted for this guitar. Not only does it offer easy handling and a modern tone, but the solid body with its appropriate weight produces a clear, articulate tone, creating a wide range of tonal variations.

- Hardware -
The lightweight and highly maneuverable Hipshot parts, also used in the Custom Series, are strategically placed throughout the instrument to directly transmit the characteristics of the wood, resulting in a more natural sound.

- Pickups -
It features a custom DiMarzio pickup, also used in the Custom Series. This pickup delivers a sharp, crisp sound with plenty of power and hum-canceling noise reduction.

- Preamp -
To achieve the ultimate tone that Kikuchi envisions, this guitar features an original preamp developed in collaboration with a world-class design manufacturer, and also used in the Custom Series. When boosted, it delivers a clear and natural tone in the low and high frequencies, and scoops the midrange, while the passive/active switching is designed to minimize the perceived difference in volume and tone.

*The image is a sample.

【SPECIFICATIONS】
Model: 4st
Body: Ash 2-Piece (Solid)
Neck : Maple, Thin Matte Nitro
Fingerboard: Rosewood
Fingerboard Radius: 12 (305R)
Nut Material: Tusq
Nut Width: 4st: 37mm
Fret: DHP24H, 22F
Scale: 864mm (34)
Body Finish: Poly
Hardware Color: Chrome
Bridge: Hipshot 20mm Spacing
Tuner: Hipshot License Ultra-Light
Pickups : DiMarzio 4HC in 60's Locations
Preamp : Kikuchi Guitars Original Preamp
Controls: Master Volume, Pickup Balancer, Passive Roll Off Tone (w/Pull Switch for Preamp Bypass), Treble/Bass Boost

Includes semi-hard case

item.description:
$3,173.02 · Brand New · Ikebe Music

Our Business Hours is Mon to Fri / 11:00 to 18:00 JPN time
We will close Sat and Sun, so we will reply to inquiry that received weekend on next week.
Your understanding will be appreciated.

■Please note
The product description is an automatic translation of the Japanese description. Please contact us if you have any questions.

-----------------------------------

[Atelier Z × IKEBE Original Pouch Gift]
We're giving away an original pouch (not for sale) created through a collaboration between Atelier Z and IKEBE!
It fits in the pocket of your gig bag and is convenient for storing maintenance tools like wrenches that tend to get scattered around!
This is a rare item that can only be obtained by those who purchase an Atelier Z bass guitar at Ikebe. Take this opportunity to find your dream Atelier Z!

Eligible products: Purchase an ATELIER Z bass guitar priced at 300,000 yen or more (including tax) and receive a free gift. *This is a limited-quantity gift, so please note that it will be discontinued once supplies run out.
*Some items, such as outlet items, sale items, reuse/second-hand items, and made-to-order items, may be excluded.
----------------------------------------
A color-order model of the flagship and ultra-classic M#245.

ATELIER Z is a long-established Japanese brand that enjoys widespread support from both professional and amateur players across a variety of genres. This particular model is the M#245, the company's flagship model widely known as a high-quality, domestically produced active JB bass. Furthermore, it boasts a backbone based on the intentions of Tomohito Aoki, and is highly regarded as a model favored by musicians.

While based on the 70s JB type, this bass incorporates a body shape with an original design that takes into consideration the prevention of the instrument tipping over, the installation of the unique low-action setting "Z Tuning System" that provides a pleasant buzz, the powerful active sound of the Bartolini XTCT (which also has a mid-cut control in the cavity), the original bridge with a substantial mass that is a characteristic of this brand, and a thick clear pickguard and pickup fence specification that allows for smooth control of thumbing and pulling when slapping, which is a specialty of this brand. The specifications and design are packed with attention to detail from a bassist's perspective. Furthermore, this instrument is made with the latest specification with an added master tone, allowing for fine nuances even when used in passive mode.

This bass boasts a dynamic and powerful bass thanks to its ash body, a pleasantly tight sound image, and a fast attack response thanks to its coated one-piece maple neck. You can enjoy the dynamic and clear sound of a 70's JB bass with a supple touch, making it truly a player's gear. Furthermore, the rich vibration produced by the full-size body and the pleasant, rustic tone resulting from the open resonance of the traditional neck construction without graphite support are also key features and appealing aspects of this model.

This instrument is a custom color order model offered by our store. It combines the popular gradient color, Pink Fade, with a white pearl pickguard. The control panel remains the conventional clear panel and is provided as an accessory rather than being pre-installed. Its distinctive sound is instantly recognizable, and the meticulous quality control that comes with basses from this workshop is one of the reasons why it captivates so many bassists. Take this opportunity to get your hands on this authentic model that will make its presence felt on stage and in the recording studio.

Weight: ≈4.05kg
S/N: #012384

【Specifications】
[Neck]
Scale: 34 inches (864 mm)
Neck Material: Maple 1Pc.
Fingerboard Material: Maple
Fingerboard Radius: 240R
Fret Size: W2.9mm/H1.2mm
Frets: 20 Frets
Nut Material: Bone
Nut Width: 38.5mm
Finish: Fingerboard&Head top/Gloss, Neck Back/Mat
[Body]
Body Material: Ash (2-piece set)
Body Shape: JB M Type (Original J Body)
Body Finish: Gloss
[Hardware]
Tuningkey: GOTOH GB528
Bridge: ATELIER Z BB419
Finish: Chrome
Pickguard: White Pearl 4Ply 2.4mm
Control panel: Acrylic Clear 3mm *Not included/Accessories
Pickup Fence: ATELIER Z ZPF-4500
Control Knobs: ATELIER Z ZAK-1500
Neck Plate: ATELIER Z 4 Point Plate
Strings: ATELIER Z Stainless Steel Strings SPS-3300 (.45 .65 .85 .105)
[Electronics]
Pickups: ATELIER Z JBZ-4
Preamp: Bartolini XTCT+Spectrum Boost
Controls: M'Vol., M.Tone, Balancer, Treble Boost, Bass Boost/sw (Active ON/OFF)
*Middle cut included
Color: FADE PINK

Gig bag included

item.title: 【used】Rickenbacker / 4003 FG 2024 4.460kg #24 45686【GIB Yokohama】【price included SHIPPING & IMPORT TAX & VAT】 — $2,857
item.description:
$2,857 · Very Good · Bass Shop Geek IN Box

Rickenbacker is one of the most traditional guitar brands, enjoying strong support from many musicians.


The 4003 is positioned as Rickenbacker's flagship model.


While the basic specifications follow the 4001, it has been updated for modern bassists, primarily focusing on hardware such as the truss rod and pickups.


The body and neck are made of maple, and the fretboard has a gloss finish, a standard feature for the brand.


It features a single double-action truss rod, a newly designed bridge, and a slimmer neck, making it a more modern update.


The greatest appeal of this model lies in its extremely thick low-mids and low-density, and its sharp, metallic attack.


Its sound possesses a mass like a steel ball.


An iconic instrument that has continued to be used in heavy rock ensembles.


Its usefulness has been proven by many great musicians.


Highly recommended.


Fret remaining is approximately 70%.


The truss rod is in good condition.

The neck is in good condition.

The body and paintwork have some minor scratches, but overall it is in good condition.

The electronics are in good condition.

No obvious modifications were observed.

Controls: Neck pickup volume, neck pickup tone, bridge pickup volume, bridge pickup tone, 3-way pickup selector

Nut width: 43.5mm

Bridge string spacing: 19.0mm

Scale length: 34 inches

Year of manufacture: 2024

Country of manufacture: USA

Basic Co., Ltd. provides a warranty against defects for products sold by the company. With the exception of some items such as consigned goods, we offer free repair (or refund if repair is not possible) for malfunctions occurring under normal use during the warranty period from the date of purchase.


The warranty period is 3 months for used items and 1 year for new items.

item.title: Ibanez ATK305-AM Standard 1995 - Amber — $520.53
item.description:
$520.53 · Very Good · James's Gear Outlet

For sale is a 1995 Korean-made Ibanez ATK305 5-string bass in excellent playing condition. Built at the highly regarded Cort factory, these early ATK models have developed a strong reputation for their rock-solid construction, huge tone, and incredible versatility.

The ATK series is often described as Ibanez’s answer to the classic Music Man-style bass, delivering massive low end, punchy mids, and clear articulation that works equally well for rock, funk, metal, and modern styles. The 3-band active EQ and powerful triple-coil pickup provide a wide range of tones — from aggressive growl to smooth, full-bodied warmth.

This bass has the feel and quality that made the mid-’90s Korean ATKs so respected among players. Comfortable neck, excellent sustain, and a tone that cuts through a mix effortlessly.

Features:

  • Made in Korea (Cort factory)
  • 1995 production
  • 5-string model
  • Active electronics
  • Triple-coil ATK pickup
  • Heavy-duty ATK bridge
  • Famous ATK punch and sustain

Condition:
Very good overall condition for its age with normal cosmetic wear from use. Fully functional and plays great. Electronics work properly and the neck feels solid and stable.

A fantastic choice for players looking for boutique-level tone and construction without boutique pricing.

Ships from Canada.
Will be packed securely with proper protection.

item.title: Dingwall D-Roc Standard 4-String - Ducati Gold Matt LTD — $2,324.57
item.description:
$2,324.57 · Brand New · Klangfarbe

Der Korpus besteht aus afrikanischem Mahagoni namens Khaya, mit einem fünfteiligen Ahornhals und einem Pau-Ferro-Griffbrett. Jede der vier Farboptionen wurde mit einer Polyurethanbeschichtung versehen, wodurch sich diese D-Roc wirklich von der Masse abhebt. Die wachsende Liste der Endorser gibt einen Hinweis darauf, wo sie sich großer Beliebtheit erfreuen – zu den aktuellen Nutzern gehören Jermaine Blandford (Jazmine Sullivan), Pete Griffin (Dethklok) und Nick Campbell (Scary Pockets). „In Bezug auf Leistung, Ausstattung, Hardware und Gesamtklang ist die D-Roc Standard ein Spitzenmodell. Dingwall hat einen Gewinner hervorgebracht, der all das Designethos und die Liebe zum Detail in sich vereint, die man von Sheldon Dingwall erwarten würde.“

PICKUP & ELEKTRONIK
Der D-Roc Standard ist mit drei passiven FD-3n-Tonabnehmern ausgestattet. Der Schlüssel zu ihrem druckvollen Klang ist die Verwendung von Neodym-Magneten; eine höhere Ausgangsleistung bedeutet einen aggressiveren Sound. Mit dem 4-Positionen-Quad-Tone-Tonabnehmerwahlschalter können Sie wählen, ob Sie nur den Steg-Tonabnehmer, den Steg- und den mittleren Tonabnehmer, nur den mittleren Tonabnehmer oder den Steg-, den mittleren und den Hals-Tonabnehmer gleichzeitig betreiben möchten. Die übrigen Regler umfassen einen passiven Tonregler und einen Master-Lautstärkeregler. „Wenn Sie eine Tonabnehmer-Einstellung auswählen und den Tonregler durchspielen, werden deutliche Klangnuancen sichtbar. Kombinieren Sie verschiedene Spielstile und wenden Sie dann die Tonabnehmer-Kombinationen an, und Sie werden keine Mangel an Klangoptionen und Flexibilität haben.“

HARDWARE
Während der D-Roc Standard 4-String über eine einstellbare Hipshot-Brücke verfügt, die speziell für Dingwall entwickelt wurde, kommt bei der 5-String-Version unsere minimalistische Brücke zum Einsatz. Diese neue Brückeneinheit wurde speziell entwickelt, um leicht, solide und einfach einzustellen zu sein (mit einem einzigen Schraubenschlüssel lassen sich sowohl die Saitenlage als auch die Intonation einstellen). Die versteckte Kugeltasche vereinfacht außerdem das Aufziehen neuer Saiten und macht den Vorgang schneller und einfacher als je zuvor. Die übrigen Hardware-Komponenten, von den gerändelten Reglern bis zur randmontierten Klinkenbuchse, sind mit geräuchertem Nickel beschichtet.

INNOVATIVE DESIGNS
Sheldon Dingwalls Vorliebe für den Bau maßgeschneiderter Gitarren geht auf seine Arbeit in der Werkstatt seines Onkels in seiner Jugend zurück. Mit 19 Jahren gründete er eine Plattenfirma und tourte anschließend mit seiner eigenen Band. Als er 1987 nach Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, Kanada, zurückkehrte, gründete er Dingwall Guitars mit einem bestimmten Ziel: die vielseitigsten Instrumente zu entwerfen und zu bauen, die die Branche je gesehen hat. Zu den angesehenen Errungenschaften des Unternehmens zählen seine exquisiten E-Bässe, die mit dem Fanned-Fret-System von Novax, proprietären Tonabnehmern, innovativer Elektronik und maßgeschneiderter Hardware ausgestattet sind. Passenderweise umfasst die Künstlerliste von Dingwall eine Reihe renommierter Bassisten aus verschiedenen Genres, darunter Leland Sklar, Adam „Nolly“ Getgood, Jacob Umansky, Stefan Lessard, Tony Levin, Pete Griffin, Mason Nagy und Jean-Michel Labadie.

Finish – Metallic Gold

Korpus – Khaya

Hals – 5-teiliges Ahorn, Bolt-On

Griffbrett – Pau Ferro

Mensur – 34" – 36,25"

Kopfplatte – Non Matching

Sattel – Graphtech Black Tusq

Pickups – Dingwall FD3n, Neodymium-Magnete

Elektronik – Passive

Regler – Master Volume, 4-Position Quad-Tone Pickup Selector, Passive Tone

Gigbag – Dingwall Padded Gigbag

item.description:
$1,114.68 · Very Good · James's Gear Outlet

2007 Fender American Jazz Bass S-1

3-Tone Sunburst • Maple Fingerboard • Includes Fender Hard Shell Case


Up for sale is a beautiful 2007 Fender American Jazz Bass featuring the sought-after S-1 switching system, classic 3-Tone Sunburst finish, and maple neck/fingerboard combination. This is a fantastic-playing and sounding USA-made Jazz Bass with the versatility and feel that made these mid-2000s American models so popular.


The S-1 switch adds extra tonal flexibility by engaging a series wiring option, giving this bass a thicker, more aggressive tone while still retaining the classic articulate Jazz Bass sound. From vintage warmth to punchy modern tones, this bass covers a lot of ground.


Condition is great overall and it has clearly been well cared for. The finish presents very nicely with only light cosmetic wear consistent with normal use. The neck feels excellent, the frets are in great shape, and everything functions exactly as it should.


Features include:

• Made in USA (2007)

• Alder body

• Maple neck with maple fingerboard

• 3-Tone Sunburst finish

• Fender S-1 switching system

• Dual Fender Jazz Bass single-coil pickups

• High-mass bridge

• Original Fender hard shell case included


This is a killer American Jazz Bass that’s equally at home in the studio, on stage, or in a collection. The maple board and sunburst combination is a timeless Fender look and always highly desirable.

Up for sale is a gorgeous Fender American Precision Bass featuring the desirable S-1 switching system and stunning Butterscotch Blonde finish. This USA-made P Bass delivers everything you’d expect from an American Fender — huge tone, exceptional playability, and timeless looks.


The S-1 switching system adds extra versatility by engaging a series-style circuit for a thicker, more powerful tone while still retaining the classic punch and authority that Precision Basses are famous for. Whether you’re after vintage warmth, aggressive rock grind, or deep studio-ready low end, this bass delivers.


The Butterscotch Blonde finish gives this instrument an incredible classic vibe and really stands out compared to the more common colors. Paired with the rosewood fingerboard, it has a premium vintage-inspired look that’s hard to beat.


Condition is very clean overall and the bass has clearly been well cared for. It plays beautifully with a comfortable neck, strong electronics, and excellent fret life remaining. A fantastic instrument for stage, studio, or collection use.
